attacking_style: Attacks on steep ramps in final 3-5km of climbs, often initiating moves before favorites respond. Studies finish profiles meticulously (2012 Balcón video analysis) to time corner-based attacks where sprint speed deficits are neutralized.
team_tactics: Frequently isolated without domestique support in Grand Tour GC battles. Movistar historically sacrificed his opportunities for Valverde (Flèche 2022 leadout). When protected, benefits from controlled tempo but team often lacks resources in decisive final kilometers.
terrain_preference: Long, steady gradients where durability prevails over explosive accelerations. Struggles on punchy finishes requiring repeated surges (lost 44s to Evenepoel 2022) and exposed on technical descents. Monument distance remains physical ceiling (Lombardia 2023 DNF).
vulnerability: Time trials create catastrophic GC losses (2.5min Dauphiné 2023, 1min+ to Roglič 2022). Cannot match explosive accelerations from top-tier climbers like Roglič and Pogačar. Late-race fatigue accumulation in 3rd week causes form collapse (Vuelta 2023 podium tumble).
key_patterns: Severe TT weakness hemorrhages 1-2.5 minutes, eliminating GC threat before mountain stages begin (4 observations)
Opportunistic bonus second hunter, bridges to attacks when GC rivals crack momentarily (3 observations)
Attacks early but lacks team support to sustain efforts, neutralized by rivals marking (5 observations)
Sharp sprint finish when defensive tactics prevail, gains seconds in reduced group sprints (3 observations)
Pre-race course reconnaissance and video study informs precise tactical execution on known climbs (2 observations)
Monument and 3rd week form deterioration, struggles with accumulation and extreme distance (4 observations)
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 Vuelta showed podium collapse from 2nd overall due to accumulation fatigue, followed by Lombardia DNF suggesting declining Monument/Grand Tour resilience at age 28+
